ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Continued To Jan-16-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-13-296691 The Berta Moses Trust and PTR-297636 The Manfred Moses Trust.
Appearances: Sonia Agee and John Koeppel for Irit Zmany (Petitioner/Beneficiary of Berta Moses). Henry Glasser for David Moses. Franchesca Callejo for and with Randolph Stein.

With respect to the Berta Moses Trust, the Court stated that a declination from Ms. Evergreen and re-notice of the petition is needed. Ms. Agee stated that Ms. Evergreen is an elderly person living in a care facility and that there is no communication. The Court also informed Counsel that it is not prepared to accept a joint accounting for both Trusts. Ms. Callejo stated that her Client understands that he needs to pull out expenses for the Manfred Moses Trust but that he will need some time, that she will list what items have been pulled out, that she can go through her file and provide all grant deeds that she has in her possession, and that she would like an itemization of documents that have not been produced. Ms. Agee stated that she has an issue with a continuance. The Berta Moses Trust (296691) was set for a one half day trial (as represented by Ms. Agee) on January 16, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Manfred Trust (297636) was set for trial (no more than two half days as represented by Mr. Glasser) on January 23, 2015 and January 30, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. All Counsel were advised that witness lists, exhibit lists and trial briefs are to be filed three days prior to the trial date.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

December 19, 2014 In re Berta Moses Trust: The two orders to show cause are off calendar. The Petition to Appoint Successor Trustee is continued to February 2, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Off Calendar

Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Off Calendar

Petition To Appoint Petitioner Irit Altschuler Zmany As Successor Trustee To The Berta Moses Trust
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Continued To Dec-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FIRST ACCOUNTING OF TRUSTEE
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Continued To Dec-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-13-296691 The Berta Moses Trust and PTR-14-297636 The Manfred Moses Testamentary Trust.

Appearances: Sonia Agee for Irit Zmany. Franchesca Callejo for Randolph Stein (Former Trustee/Trustee). Henry Glasser for Beneficiaries of Manfred Moses.

In re The Berta Moses Trust: Ms. Agee stated they just recently received something from Ms. Callejo from 1980, that the request for information was from the date of settlement, that Mr. Stein has failed to execute deeds regarding the property and that there are no tax documents. Ms. Callejo stated the Parties can not agree on how expenditures are to be allocated, that her Client has not filed taxes, that both matters need to come together, that no matter what the Trustee does it will not be acceptable to both sides and that she has not had the opportunity to discuss the issue of transferring funds to his personal account with her Client. Ms. Agee stated that both sides agree that the settlement of April 2011 should be followed, that it is very clear as to how expenditures are to be paid, that the accountings should be as of the date of settlement, that she will petition for surcharge and that she would like separate accountings. Ms. Callejo requested at least 60-days. Mr. Glasser requested that The Manfred Moses Trust be set for trial. Berta's Trust was continued to December 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Stein is ordered to be present. Manfred's Trust was also continued to December 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trial setting.

Judge: John Dearman. Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to removal of Mr. Stein, appointment of Ms. Zmany, continuing the matter to allow time for Mr. Stein to complete the accounting, provide documents and respond to the Probate Examiner's notes, whether to put the contempt and surcharge issues over, etc. The Court wants a revised order from Ms. Agee setting forth specific documents requested and a separate proposed order regarding appointment of Ms. Zmany as temporary successor trustee. The deadline for Mr. Stein to produce documents is October 3, 2014. All matters were continued to October 20, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Stein will appear by court call.

Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Continued To Sep-22-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sonia Agee & John Koeppel for (petitioner) Irit Zmany. Dennis Vann for Randolph Stein.

Mr. Vann reported that he has forwarded the court's minutes as well as the order to Mr. Stein with no response, and that he has no financial records other than what was in the previous accounting. He further reported that his communications with Mr. Stein have been via email. Ms. Agee requests that Mr. Stein be removed as trustee and that Irit Zmany be appointed, that Mr. Stein turn over the assets and that the petitioner will request a surcharge for expenses.
The court asks Ms. Agee to file a petition for successor trustee and that further information on the surcharge will be needed. The court ordered that Mr. Stein be removed as trustee and to compel an accounting and Ms. Agee is to submit a proposed order to such. The court will issue an order to show cause re contempt to Mr. Stein with a return date of September 22, 2014. All remaining matters were continued to September 22, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Jun-23-2014 Continued To Jul-28-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sonia Agee and John Koeppel for Petitioner. Dennis Vann appeared by telephone for Randolph Stein.

Mr. Vann stated that the Burnette property has not been distributed, that it was supposed to have been transferred from the Husband's Trust to the Berta Moses Trust and then transferred to Ms. Zmany, and that neither transfer has occurred. Mr. Vann also stated that his Client told him that he had the transfers, that he told his Client to execute them and provide them to opposing Counsel, that it has not been done and expressed his frustration. The Court directed Counsel for the Petitioner to prepare an order suspending Mr. Stein's powers and authority as Trustee pending his response to an order to show cause. An Order to Show Cause Re Suspension will be issued to Randolph Stein to appear on July 28, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A response/opposition to the Order to Show Cause if any is to be filed no later than July 7, 2014. A reply if any is to be filed by July 16, 2014. The Petition to Compel Accounting, etc. and the First Account are also continued to July 28, 2014.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-02-2013 Off Calendar

Petition To:1) Compel Accounting; 2) Compel Distribution; And 3) Order Surcharge.
Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Continued To Dec-02-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: John Koeppel for Irit Zmany (Petitioner). Dennis Vann for and with Randolph Stein (Trustee/Respondent).

Mr. Vann stated that Mr. Stein has not filed the accounting, that he has filed a Response to the Petition, that proof of distribution has been made and that he will need 30 days to file the accounting. Discussions were held with regard to file an inventory of assets and whether a Probate Referee is need to appraise the real property. Mr. Koeppel stated that he is concerned with regard to over $300,000.00 rent has been collected, taxes are not being paid and he is not sure if the property is being maintain. He requested for an order distributing the property immediately, that an inventory and accounting be provided, or in the alternative, the Trust be frozen, that court approval is required to make any payment, that payment of attorney's fees is not trust expenses and to bring the trust under court supervision. Mr. Vann stated that Mr. Stein agreed to the distribution of the real property, that he is concerned with the unsecured escape tax which is about $50,000.00. Mr. Koeppel agreed that his client will pay the escape tax due on the property except for the delinquency. The Court ordered the distribution of the property to the beneficiary with unsecured tax not a lien on the property that counsel to meet and confer with regard to the accounting and inventory. The matter was continued to December 2, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of an accounting with an OSC attached. If the accounting is filed and a hearing date set, the status will go off calendar. Mr. Koeppel to prepare an order and send it to Mr. Vann for approval as to form.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Continued To Dec-02-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To:1) Compel Accounting; 2) Compel Distribution; And 3) Order Surcharge.
Off Calendar Oct-21-2013 Continued To Nov-04-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sonia Agee for Irit Zmany (Petitioner).

Ms. Agee stated that she did not receive the accounting from Mr. Stein, that she checked the docket and that nothing has been filed. Ms. Agee also stated that the receipts have not been filed and that the property has not been distributed. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Randolph Stein to appear on November 4, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he has not been filed his response, the accounting petition and the receipts as ordered by Judge Wiss at the September 16, 2013 hearing. The Petition to Compel Accounting; Compel Distribution; Order Surcharge is also continued to that date.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition To:1) Compel Accounting; 2) Compel Distribution; And 3) Order Surcharge.
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Continued To Oct-21-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sonia Agee and Wendy Krog for Irit Zmany (Petitioner). Randolph Stein in Propria Persona.

Mr. Stein stated that all six of the specific pecuniary gifts were made and that he has receipts for them. The Court stated that it would be helpful to have the receipts filed so that it can determine who needs to receive notice. Mr. Stein also stated that there was an offer regarding distribution, that the original plan was to sell the property to pay expenses, that this Trust was entangled with another Trust, that the settlement was to extricate certain Parties from this Trust and spoke of outstanding liabilities. Ms. Agee stated that only one property (Barnette property) was to be sold and the other property was to be distributed, that they have been asking for an accounting for some time, that there are arrears in property taxes and that they have not received any information regarding how the income is being spent. Mr. Stein stated the matter should be continued for a response, that the accounting will be complicated, that there is a business involved, that he was unaware that the petition was filed in April and that his address has changed. Mr. Stein needs to file his response and accounting petition pursuant to Probate Code by October 16, 2013. Ms. Agee agreed to an accounting as of April 17, 2011. The accounting petition will cover the period of April 17, 2011 through September 30, 2013. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust, etc. was continued to October 21, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Ex Parte Application To Advance Hearing On Petition To (1) Compel Accounting; (2) Compel Distribution; And (3) Order Surcharge
Off Calendar Aug-23-2013 Denied

Petition To:1) Compel Accounting; 2) Compel Distribution; And 3) Order Surcharge.
